title: Stack Project Configuration
description: Per-project configuration for Claude Code Stack. Lives at <project>/.claude/stack-config.json. Created by /project-init, edited by /default-edit project and shortcut skills.

stack_version.description: Semantic version of the stack this config was created against.

purpose.description: One-line description of what this project is for.

orchestration_mode.description: v1.1: How foreman dispatches subagents. main-thread is trusted default; agent-teams uses Anthropic's experimental Agent Teams; hybrid mixes (main-thread critical path + parallel review/audit via teams); dynamic-workflows uses Opus 4.8's research-preview workflow runtime for read-only fan-out, gated behind /cost-gate (v1.2).

strict_mode.description: If true, foreman refuses to dispatch in projects without stack-config.json.

domain_mode.description: Activates extra safety rules in foreman. null means no special mode.

active_subagents.description: Subagents enabled for this project. Empty array means use tier defaults.

model_overrides.description: Per-subagent model overrides. Keys are subagent names; values are model identifiers from model-routing.json.

review.description: ADR-030: adversarial-review transport settings (Tier 2+). Read by scripts/lib/openai-review.sh.

codex_transport.description: How the OpenAI/GPT-5.5 review family is reached: 'api' (default — OpenAI API via oair_call) or 'cli' (codex exec with automatic API fallback). Precedence: env REVIEW_CODEX_TRANSPORT > this > default 'api'.

brevity.description: v1.2: Per-project tuning for the brevity-drift UserPromptSubmit hook (hooks/brevity-drift.sh). Absent -> the hook uses its built-in defaults (enabled, 120 words / 6 sentences). Any field missing or non-numeric falls back to that default.

enabled.description: If false, the brevity-drift hook stays silent for this project (e.g. docs- or prose-heavy repos).

word_budget.description: Word count above which the assistant's last response triggers a brevity correction.

sentence_budget.description: Sentence count above which the assistant's last response triggers a brevity correction.

session_prefs.description: v1.2: Project-level default session preferences offered by /session, /goodmorning, /project-init. Live session overrides are written to ~/.claude/session-state/current-prefs.json; this block is the project fallback. Precedence: session-state > this > stack-defaults.session_prefs_defaults > built-in.

communication_style.description: Maps to the brevity-drift word/sentence budgets.

model_effort.description: Opus 4.8 effort preference for the session (5-point, ADR-022). Legacy fast/balanced/max remain valid (subset).

orchestration_mode.description: Session orchestration override; falls back to top-level orchestration_mode if unset.

passive_suggest.description: When true, dispatch-nudge.sh appends a pointer to the recommend-capabilities engine. Set to false to silence the recommender pointer while keeping the /foreman//dispatch routing nudge.

simple_talk.description: v1.3 (ADR-032): plainness/wording modifier, orthogonal to communication_style (which sets length). off = no change; plain = plain words, no jargon; caveman = terse caveman phrasing. Injected each turn by hooks/simple-talk.sh.

model_fit_receipt.description: v1.3 (ADR-033): post-session model-fit receipt. on (default) = at session end, print one retrospective line naming actual cost/model, the session's workload shape, and a cheaper/stronger alternative if the ratio-based signal is clear. off = no receipt; the main-session accrual hook (hooks/model-fit-turn.sh) writes nothing.

loop_policy.description: Governance for autonomous loops (ADR-019). Defaults ship safe; tier raises the ceiling.

max_recursion_depth.description: ADVISORY in Phase 1: the runtime does not enforce fan-out/recursion depth; max_iterations is the hard cap. Phase 2 will enforce this.

per_run_budget_usd.description: Between-iteration advisory cap in Phase 1: cost is accrued from subagent-runs.jsonl at each Stop-hook invocation and compared before the next iteration. Does NOT stop a within-iteration runaway (Phase 2 live monitor closes that gap).

workflow_roster.description: v1.2: Controls hooks/workflow-roster-check.sh. 'warn' (default): write-heavy Workflow runs naming no roster agentType emit a non-blocking system-reminder. 'block': such runs are denied (permissionDecision:deny). 'off': no warn/deny, but the run is still logged. Absent or invalid -> 'warn'. No auto-escalation from strict_mode/sensitivity (ADR-016).
